 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-oyg6","title":"Implement public status page and incident management","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-cr0q.

## Task
Create a public status page showing service health and incidents.

## Why This Matters
- Transparency with users
- Reduce support tickets
- Professional SaaS expectation
- Incident communication

## Features

### 1. Status Page
- Overall system status
- Individual component status
- Uptime percentage (30/90 days)
- Response time metrics

### 2. Components to Monitor
| Component | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| Web App | Main application |
| API | REST API endpoints |
| Authentication | Login/signup |
| Database | Data operations |
| Search | Search functionality |
| Payments | Stripe integration |
| Email | Transactional emails |

### 3. Status Levels
- Operational (green)
- Degraded Performance (yellow)
- Partial Outage (orange)
- Major Outage (red)
- Maintenance (blue)

### 4. Incident Management
- Create incident
- Update incident status
- Post-incident report
- Incident history
- Subscribe to updates

### 5. Scheduled Maintenance
- Announce maintenance windows
- Countdown to maintenance
- Automatic status change
- Completion notification

## Implementation

### Status Check
```typescript
interface ComponentStatus {
  name: string;
  status: 'operational' | 'degraded' | 'partial' | 'major' | 'maintenance';
  latency?: number;
  lastChecked: Date;
}

async function checkComponentHealth(component: string): Promise<ComponentStatus> {
  switch (component) {
    case 'database':
      return await checkDatabaseHealth();
    case 'api':
      return await checkAPIHealth();
    // ... other components
  }
}
```

### Database Schema
```typescript
export const incidents = pgTable('incidents', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  title: text('title').notNull(),
  status: text('status').notNull(), // investigating, identified, monitoring, resolved
  impact: text('impact').notNull(), // none, minor, major, critical
  affectedComponents: text('affected_components').array(),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
  resolvedAt: timestamp('resolved_at'),
});

export const incidentUpdates = pgTable('incident_updates', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  incidentId: uuid('incident_id').references(() => incidents.id).notNull(),
  status: text('status').notNull(),
  message: text('message').notNull(),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
});

export const statusSubscribers = pgTable('status_subscribers', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  email: text('email').notNull().unique(),
  confirmedAt: timestamp('confirmed_at'),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
});
```

## Public Pages
- /status - Main status page
- /status/history - Incident history
- /status/subscribe - Email subscription

## Admin Pages
- /admin/status - Manage status
- /admin/incidents - Manage incidents
- /admin/maintenance - Schedule maintenance

## Integration
- Integrate with uptime monitoring (nelm)
- Automatic incident creation on outage
- Webhook to Slack/Discord

## Files to Create
- src/app/(public)/status/page.tsx
- src/app/api/status/route.ts
- src/app/(app)/admin/status/page.tsx
- src/lib/status/health-checks.ts
- src/lib/status/incidents.ts

## Success Criteria
- Status page always accessible
- Real-time status updates
- Incident communication clear
- Subscription system works

 {"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-p8b6","title":"E2E Tests: Public Status Page","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-2qj5.

## Task
Create E2E tests for the public status page.

## Test Scenarios
1. **Status Page Display**
   - Page loads correctly
   - Current status shows
   - All services listed
   - Uptime percentages display

2. **Incident History**
   - Past incidents shown
   - Incident timeline works
   - Status updates visible
   - Resolution time displayed

3. **Subscribe to Updates**
   - Email subscription works
   - Webhook subscription works
   - Unsubscribe works

4. **Admin Incident Management**
   - Create incident (admin)
   - Update incident status
   - Resolve incident
   - Post-mortem publishing

## Logging Requirements
- Log page views
- Log subscription events
- Log incident state changes
- Screenshot status states

## Files to Create
- e2e/status/page.spec.ts
- e2e/status/subscribe.spec.ts
- e2e/admin/incidents.spec.ts

## Success Criteria
- Status page accurate
- Real-time updates work
- Subscription reliable
- Admin tools functional

-{"id":"jeffreysprompts.com-p9c1","title":"Implement user suspension and ban workflow","description":"## Origin
Moved from jeffreysprompts_premium jeffreysprompts_premium-0798.

## Task
Implement structured workflow for user suspensions and bans.

## Why This Matters
- Handle violations consistently
- Progressive discipline
- Document actions
- Enable reinstatement

## Discipline Levels

### 1. Warning
- First offense for minor issues
- No access restriction
- Logged in history
- Educational message

### 2. Temporary Suspension (1-30 days)
- Moderate violations
- Cannot access account
- Content hidden
- Auto-reinstates

### 3. Indefinite Suspension
- Serious violations
- Manual review required
- Appeal possible
- Content preserved

### 4. Permanent Ban
- Severe/repeat violations
- Account deleted
- Content removed
- IP tracked (optional)

## Implementation

### Moderation Actions Table
```typescript
export const moderationActions = pgTable('moderation_actions', {
  id: uuid('id').primaryKey().defaultRandom(),
  
  // Target
  userId: uuid('user_id').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  contentId: uuid('content_id'), // If content-specific
  contentType: text('content_type'),
  
  // Action
  actionType: text('action_type').notNull(), // warning, suspension, ban
  severity: integer('severity').notNull(), // 1-4
  reason: text('reason').notNull(),
  internalNotes: text('internal_notes'),
  
  // Duration (for suspensions)
  startsAt: timestamp('starts_at').defaultNow(),
  endsAt: timestamp('ends_at'),
  
  // Tracking
  performedBy: uuid('performed_by').references(() => userProfiles.id).notNull(),
  createdAt: timestamp('created_at').defaultNow(),
  
  // Reversal
  reversedAt: timestamp('reversed_at'),
  reversedBy: uuid('reversed_by'),
  reversalReason: text('reversal_reason'),
});
```

### User Status Check
```typescript
async function checkUserStatus(userId: string): Promise<UserStatus> {
  const activeActions = await db.select()
    .from(moderationActions)
    .where(and(
      eq(moderationActions.userId, userId),
      isNull(moderationActions.reversedAt),
      or(
        isNull(moderationActions.endsAt),
        gt(moderationActions.endsAt, new Date())
      )
    ))
    .orderBy(desc(moderationActions.severity));
  
  if (activeActions.length === 0) return { status: 'active' };
  
  const mostSevere = activeActions[0];
  return {
    status: mostSevere.actionType,
    reason: mostSevere.reason,
    endsAt: mostSevere.endsAt,
  };
}
```

### Enforcement
- Check status on login
- Block suspended users
- Show suspension message
- Redirect to appeal page

## Admin UI
- User lookup with history
- Take action button
- History timeline
- Reinstate option

## Notifications
- Email when action taken
- Appeal instructions included
- Reinstatement notification

## Files to Create
- src/lib/db/schema/moderation.ts
- src/lib/moderation/enforcement.ts
- src/app/(auth)/suspended/page.tsx
- src/app/(app)/admin/users/[id]/moderate/page.tsx

## Success Criteria
- Actions applied correctly
- Enforcement prevents access
- History tracked
- Reinstatement works
